[[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ}}, {{ill|Yoshino District, Nara|ja|еҗүйҮҺйғЎ}}, {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘}} Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|жё©жіү}} located in {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ}}, Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|2011 Typhoon Talas|ja|е№іжҲҗ23е№ҙеҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ era|ja|дә«дҝқ}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}}

On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|жё©жіү}} located in {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ}}, Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ}}. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

[[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ}}, {{ill|Yoshino District, Nara|ja|еҗүйҮҺйғЎ}}, {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘}} Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|жё©жіү}} located in {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ}}, Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|2011 Typhoon Talas|ja|е№іжҲҗ23rd year еҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ era|ja|дә«дҝқ}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}}

The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=draft|12=draft}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|2011 Typhoon Talas|ja|е№іжҲҗ23rd year еҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ era|ja|дә«дҝқ|12=draft|12=draft}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=draft|12=draft}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…|12=draft|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}}

In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ era|ja|дә«дҝқ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}}

{{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshino District, Nara|ja|еҗүйҮҺйғЎ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|жё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas|ja|е№іжҲҗ23е№ҙеҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·|12=draft}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ era|ja|дә«дҝқ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}}

Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}}

{{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshino District, Nara|ja|еҗүйҮҺйғЎ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|жё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas|ja|е№іжҲҗ23е№ҙеҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·|12=draft}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ era|ja|дә«дҝқ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}}

{{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshino District, Nara|ja|еҗүйҮҺйғЎ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|жё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas|ja|е№іжҲҗ23е№ҙеҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·|12=draft}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ era|ja|дә«дҝқ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}}

It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas|ja|е№іжҲҗ23е№ҙеҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·|12=draft}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ era|ja|дә«дҝқ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}}

Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}}

{{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshino District, Nara|ja|еҗүйҮҺйғЎ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|жё©жіү|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas|ja|е№іжҲҗ23е№ҙеҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·|12=draft}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ era|ja|дә«дҝқ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}}

On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}}

{{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshino District, Nara|ja|еҗүйҮҺйғЎ|12=draft}}, {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘|12=draft}} Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=draft}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|жё©жіү|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ|12=draft}}, Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=draft}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=draft}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas|ja|е№іжҲҗ23е№ҙеҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·|12=draft}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ era|ja|дә«дҝқ|12=draft}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=draft}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=draft}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ|12=draft}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·|12=draft}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=draft}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…|12=draft}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|12=draft}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…|12=draft}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ|12=draft}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}}

{{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ}}, {{ill|Yoshino District, Nara|ja|еҗүйҮҺйғЎ}}, {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘}} Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]

The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas|ja|е№іжҲҗ23е№ҙеҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ era|ja|дә«дҝқ}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°}}. {{ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®жё©жіүең° |еҗҚз§° = дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү |з”»еғҸ =Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG |гӮігғЎгғігғҲ =дёҠж№Ҝе·қжІҝгҒ„гҒ®йңІеӨ©йўЁе‘Ӯ |жүҖеңЁең° = [[еҘҲиүҜзңҢ]][[еҗүйҮҺйғЎ]][[еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘]]еҮәи°· |з·ҜеәҰеәҰ = 33|з·ҜеәҰеҲҶ = 56|з·ҜеәҰз§’ = 2 |зөҢеәҰеәҰ = 135|зөҢеәҰеҲҶ = 43|зөҢеәҰз§’ = 54 |дәӨйҖҡгӮўгӮҜгӮ»гӮ№ = [[#гӮўгӮҜгӮ»гӮ№]]гҒ®зҜҖеҸӮз…§ |жіүиіӘ = гғҠгғҲгғӘгӮҰгғ  - [[зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү]] |жіүжё© =76.6 |ж№§еҮәйҮҸ =124L/min пјҲеӢ•еҠӣжҸҡж№Ҝпјү |pH =8.3 |ж¶ІжҖ§гҒ®еҲҶйЎһ =ејұгӮўгғ«гӮ«гғӘжҖ§ |жөёйҖҸең§гҒ®еҲҶйЎһ = |е®ҝжіҠж–ҪиЁӯж•° = 1 |з·ҸеҸҺе®№дәәе“Ўж•° = |е№ҙй–“жөҙе®ўж•° = |зөұиЁҲе№ҙеәҰ = |еӨ–йғЁгғӘгғігӮҜ =[http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘иҰіе…үеҚ”дјҡ]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ еҚҒжҙҘе·қ дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіүж—…йӨЁ зҘһж№ҜиҚҳ] |зү№иЁҳдәӢй … = }} '''дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү'''пјҲгҒӢгҒҝгӮҶгҒҠгӮ“гҒӣгӮ“пјүгҒҜгҖҒ[[еҘҲиүҜзңҢ]][[еҗүйҮҺйғЎ]][[еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘]]еҮәи°·гҒ«гҒӮгӮӢ[[жё©жіү]]гҖӮ == жіүиіӘ == * гғҠгғҲгғӘгӮҰгғ  - [[зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү]] **гҖҖжәҗжіүжё©еәҰ76еәҰгҖӮж№ҜгҒҜз„ЎиүІйҖҸжҳҺгҖҒгҒӢгҒҷгҒӢгҒ«зЎ«й»„иҮӯгҖӮйЈІжіүеҸҜгҖӮжәҗжіү[[жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—]]пјҲеӯЈзҜҖгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠеҠ ж°ҙгҒ«гӮҲгӮӢж№Ҝжё©иӘҝж•ҙгҒӮгӮҠпјүгҖӮжіүиіӘгғҮгғјгӮҝгҒҜзҘһж№ҜиҚҳгҒ®гӮӮгҒ® == жё©жіүең° == [[ж—…йӨЁ]] гҖҢзҘһж№ҜиҚҳгҖҚгҒЁж—Ҙеё°гӮҠжё©жіүгҖҢжІіеҺҹгҒ®ж№ҜгҖҚгҒ®гҒҝе–¶жҘӯгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮзҘһж№ҜиҚҳгҒҜдёҠж№Ҝе·қжІҝгҒ„гҒ«йңІеӨ©йўЁе‘ӮгӮ’жҢҒгҒЎгҖҒж—Ҙеё°гӮҠе…ҘжөҙгӮӮеҸҜиғҪгҖӮгҖҢ[[ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ]]гҖҚгҒ®дјҡе“ЎгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮ [[ж—Ҙеё°гӮҠе…Ҙжөҙж–ҪиЁӯ]]гҖҢеҮәи°·жё©жіүе…¬иЎҶжөҙе ҙгҒӨгӮӢгҒӨгӮӢд№ғж№ҜгҖҚгҒҜгҖҒ2011е№ҙгҒ®[[е№іжҲҗ23е№ҙеҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·|еҸ°йўЁ12еҸ·ж°ҙе®і]]гҒ§иў«зҒҪгҒ—й–үйӨЁгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ д»ҘеүҚгҒҜ[[ж°‘е®ҝ]]гӮӮгҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҖӮдёҠж№Ҝе·қжІҝгҒ„гҒ«жІіеҺҹгӮ’жҺҳгӮӢгҒЁжё©жіүгҒҢж№§гҒҸе ҙжүҖпјҲ[[йҮҺж№Ҝ]]пјүгӮӮгҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҒҢгҖҒ2011е№ҙгҒ«иў«зҒҪгҒ—гҒҰй–үйҺ–гҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҹгҖӮ2017е№ҙгҒ«йңІеӨ©йўЁе‘ӮгҖҢжІіеҺҹгҒ®ж№ҜгҖҚгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰгғӘгғӢгғҘгғјгӮўгғ«гӮӘгғјгғ—гғігҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ == жӯҙеҸІ == й–Ӣж№ҜгҒҜ[[дә«дҝқ]]е№ҙй–“пјҲ[[1716е№ҙ]] - [[1736е№ҙ]]пјүгҒЁиЁҖгӮҸгӮҢгӮӢгҖӮ [[1985е№ҙ]]пјҲжҳӯе’Ң60е№ҙпјү[[3жңҲ19ж—Ҙ]]гҖҒ[[ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү]]гҖҒ[[еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү]]гҒЁгҒЁгӮӮгҒ«гҖҢеҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіүйғ·гҖҚгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰ[[еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°]]гҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгҖӮ [[2004е№ҙ]]пјҲе№іжҲҗ16е№ҙпјү[[6жңҲ28ж—Ҙ]]гҖҒеҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘еҪ№е ҙгҒҢгҖҒжқ‘еҶ…гҒ®жё©жіүгҒ®гҖҢ100%жәҗжіү[[жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—]]е®ЈиЁҖгҖҚгӮ’зҷәиЎЁгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ == гӮўгӮҜгӮ»гӮ№ == * и»Ҡпјҡ[[дә”жўқеёӮ]]гғ»[[ж–°е®®еёӮ]]гҒӢгӮү[[еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·]]гҖҒеҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘е№іи°·и•Ёе°ҫпјҲгғҜгғ©гғ“е°ҫдәӨе·®зӮ№пјүгӮҲгӮҠ[[еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·]]зөҢз”ұгҖҒ[[еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ]]гҖӮ * гғҗгӮ№пјҡ[[иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“|иҝ‘йү„]][[еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…]]гҖҒ[[иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|JR]][[дә”жқЎй§… (еҘҲиүҜзңҢ)|дә”жқЎй§…]]гҖҒJR[[ж–°е®®й§…]]гӮҲгӮҠ[[еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ]]пјҲе…«жңЁж–°е®®з·ҡгҖҒеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡпјү - гҖҢеҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіүгҖҚеҒңз•ҷжүҖгҒ§еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘е–¶гғҗгӮ№пјҲдёҠж№Ҝе·қз·ҡгҖҒеҜәеһЈеҶ…з·ҡпјүгҒ«д№—жҸӣ - гҖҢе°ҸеҺҹеҸЈгҖҚеҒңз•ҷжүҖдёӢи»ҠеҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘е–¶гғҗгӮ№дёҠж№Ҝе·қгғ»еҜәеһЈеҶ…з·ҡгҒҜйҒӢи»ўжң¬ж•°гҒҢе°‘гҒӘгҒ„гҒҹгӮҒгҖҒеҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіүеҒңз•ҷжүҖгҒӢгӮүгҒ®гӮҝгӮҜгӮ·гғјеҸҲгҒҜж—…йӨЁйҖҒиҝҺгғҗгӮ№еҲ©з”ЁгҒҢеӨҡгҒ„гҖӮ == и„ҡжіЁ == {{Reflist}} == еӨ–йғЁгғӘгғігӮҜ == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘иҰіе…үеҚ”дјҡ > дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ еҚҒжҙҘе·қ дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіүж—…йӨЁ зҘһж№ҜиҚҳ] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html жІіеҺҹгҒ®ж№Ҝ] - еҚҒжҙҘе·қиҰіе…үеҚ”дјҡ {{жё©жіү}} {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒӢгҒҝгӮҶгҒҠгӮ“гҒӣгӮ“}} [[Category:еҘҲиүҜзңҢгҒ®жё©жіү]] [[Category:еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°]] [[Category:еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘гҒ®ең°зҗҶ]]

{{ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®жё©жіүең° |еҗҚз§° = дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү |з”»еғҸ =Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG |гӮігғЎгғігғҲ =дёҠж№Ҝе·қжІҝгҒ„гҒ®йңІеӨ©йўЁе‘Ӯ |жүҖеңЁең° = [[еҘҲиүҜзңҢ]][[еҗүйҮҺйғЎ]][[еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘]]еҮәи°· |з·ҜеәҰеәҰ = 33|з·ҜеәҰеҲҶ = 56|з·ҜеәҰз§’ = 2 |зөҢеәҰеәҰ = 135|зөҢеәҰеҲҶ = 43|зөҢеәҰз§’ = 54 |дәӨйҖҡгӮўгӮҜгӮ»гӮ№ = [[#гӮўгӮҜгӮ»гӮ№]]гҒ®зҜҖеҸӮз…§ |жіүиіӘ = гғҠгғҲгғӘгӮҰгғ  - [[зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү]] |жіүжё© =76.6 |ж№§еҮәйҮҸ =124L/min пјҲеӢ•еҠӣжҸҡж№Ҝпјү |pH =8.3 |ж¶ІжҖ§гҒ®еҲҶйЎһ =ејұгӮўгғ«гӮ«гғӘжҖ§ |жөёйҖҸең§гҒ®еҲҶйЎһ = |е®ҝжіҠж–ҪиЁӯж•° = 1 |з·ҸеҸҺе®№дәәе“Ўж•° = |е№ҙй–“жөҙе®ўж•° = |зөұиЁҲе№ҙеәҰ = |еӨ–йғЁгғӘгғігӮҜ =[http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘иҰіе…үеҚ”дјҡ]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ еҚҒжҙҘе·қ дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіүж—…йӨЁ зҘһж№ҜиҚҳ] |зү№иЁҳдәӢй … = }} '''дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү'''пјҲгҒӢгҒҝгӮҶгҒҠгӮ“гҒӣгӮ“пјүгҒҜгҖҒ[[еҘҲиүҜзңҢ]][[еҗүйҮҺйғЎ]][[еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘]]еҮәи°·гҒ«гҒӮгӮӢ[[жё©жіү]]гҖӮ == жіүиіӘ == * гғҠгғҲгғӘгӮҰгғ  - [[зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү]] **гҖҖжәҗжіүжё©еәҰ76еәҰгҖӮж№ҜгҒҜз„ЎиүІйҖҸжҳҺгҖҒгҒӢгҒҷгҒӢгҒ«зЎ«й»„иҮӯгҖӮйЈІжіүеҸҜгҖӮжәҗжіү[[жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—]]пјҲеӯЈзҜҖгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠеҠ ж°ҙгҒ«гӮҲгӮӢж№Ҝжё©иӘҝж•ҙгҒӮгӮҠпјүгҖӮжіүиіӘгғҮгғјгӮҝгҒҜзҘһж№ҜиҚҳгҒ®гӮӮгҒ® == жё©жіүең° == [[ж—…йӨЁ]] гҖҢзҘһж№ҜиҚҳгҖҚгҒЁж—Ҙеё°гӮҠжё©жіүгҖҢжІіеҺҹгҒ®ж№ҜгҖҚгҒ®гҒҝе–¶жҘӯгҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮзҘһж№ҜиҚҳгҒҜдёҠж№Ҝе·қжІҝгҒ„гҒ«йңІеӨ©йўЁе‘ӮгӮ’жҢҒгҒЎгҖҒж—Ҙеё°гӮҠе…ҘжөҙгӮӮеҸҜиғҪгҖӮгҖҢ[[ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ]]гҖҚгҒ®дјҡе“ЎгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҖӮ [[ж—Ҙеё°гӮҠе…Ҙжөҙж–ҪиЁӯ]]гҖҢеҮәи°·жё©жіүе…¬иЎҶжөҙе ҙгҒӨгӮӢгҒӨгӮӢд№ғж№ҜгҖҚгҒҜгҖҒ2011е№ҙгҒ®[[е№іжҲҗ23е№ҙеҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·|еҸ°йўЁ12еҸ·ж°ҙе®і]]гҒ§иў«зҒҪгҒ—й–үйӨЁгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ д»ҘеүҚгҒҜ[[ж°‘е®ҝ]]гӮӮгҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҖӮдёҠж№Ҝе·қжІҝгҒ„гҒ«жІіеҺҹгӮ’жҺҳгӮӢгҒЁжё©жіүгҒҢж№§гҒҸе ҙжүҖпјҲ[[йҮҺж№Ҝ]]пјүгӮӮгҒӮгҒЈгҒҹгҒҢгҖҒ2011е№ҙгҒ«иў«зҒҪгҒ—гҒҰй–үйҺ–гҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гҒҹгҖӮ2017е№ҙгҒ«йңІеӨ©йўЁе‘ӮгҖҢжІіеҺҹгҒ®ж№ҜгҖҚгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰгғӘгғӢгғҘгғјгӮўгғ«гӮӘгғјгғ—гғігҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ == жӯҙеҸІ == й–Ӣж№ҜгҒҜ[[дә«дҝқ]]е№ҙй–“пјҲ[[1716е№ҙ]] - [[1736е№ҙ]]пјүгҒЁиЁҖгӮҸгӮҢгӮӢгҖӮ [[1985е№ҙ]]пјҲжҳӯе’Ң60е№ҙпјү[[3жңҲ19ж—Ҙ]]гҖҒ[[ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү]]гҖҒ[[еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү]]гҒЁгҒЁгӮӮгҒ«гҖҢеҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіүйғ·гҖҚгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰ[[еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°]]гҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҹгҖӮ [[2004е№ҙ]]пјҲе№іжҲҗ16е№ҙпјү[[6жңҲ28ж—Ҙ]]гҖҒеҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘еҪ№е ҙгҒҢгҖҒжқ‘еҶ…гҒ®жё©жіүгҒ®гҖҢ100%жәҗжіү[[жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—]]е®ЈиЁҖгҖҚгӮ’зҷәиЎЁгҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ == гӮўгӮҜгӮ»гӮ№ == * и»Ҡпјҡ[[дә”жўқеёӮ]]гғ»[[ж–°е®®еёӮ]]гҒӢгӮү[[еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·]]гҖҒеҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘е№іи°·и•Ёе°ҫпјҲгғҜгғ©гғ“е°ҫдәӨе·®зӮ№пјүгӮҲгӮҠ[[еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·]]зөҢз”ұгҖҒ[[еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ]]гҖӮ * гғҗгӮ№пјҡ[[иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“|иҝ‘йү„]][[еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…]]гҖҒ[[иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|JR]][[дә”жқЎй§… (еҘҲиүҜзңҢ)|дә”жқЎй§…]]гҖҒJR[[ж–°е®®й§…]]гӮҲгӮҠ[[еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ]]пјҲе…«жңЁж–°е®®з·ҡгҖҒеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡпјү - гҖҢеҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіүгҖҚеҒңз•ҷжүҖгҒ§еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘е–¶гғҗгӮ№пјҲдёҠж№Ҝе·қз·ҡгҖҒеҜәеһЈеҶ…з·ҡпјүгҒ«д№—жҸӣ - гҖҢе°ҸеҺҹеҸЈгҖҚеҒңз•ҷжүҖдёӢи»ҠеҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘е–¶гғҗгӮ№дёҠж№Ҝе·қгғ»еҜәеһЈеҶ…з·ҡгҒҜйҒӢи»ўжң¬ж•°гҒҢе°‘гҒӘгҒ„гҒҹгӮҒгҖҒеҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіүеҒңз•ҷжүҖгҒӢгӮүгҒ®гӮҝгӮҜгӮ·гғјеҸҲгҒҜж—…йӨЁйҖҒиҝҺгғҗгӮ№еҲ©з”ЁгҒҢеӨҡгҒ„гҖӮ == и„ҡжіЁ == {{Reflist}} == еӨ–йғЁгғӘгғігӮҜ == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘иҰіе…үеҚ”дјҡ > дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ еҚҒжҙҘе·қ дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіүж—…йӨЁ зҘһж№ҜиҚҳ] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html жІіеҺҹгҒ®ж№Ҝ] - еҚҒжҙҘе·қиҰіе…үеҚ”дјҡ {{жё©жіү}} {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒӢгҒҝгӮҶгҒҠгӮ“гҒӣгӮ“}} [[Category:еҘҲиүҜзңҢгҒ®жё©жіү]] [[Category:еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°]] [[Category:еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘гҒ®ең°зҗҶ]]

[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|жё©жіү|1=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ}}, Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas|ja|е№іжҲҗ23е№ҙеҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ era|ja|дә«дҝқ}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New-]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kami-Yu Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}}

On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=draft|12=draft}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…|12=draft|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|жё©жіү|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=draft|12=draft}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=draft|12=draft}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas|ja|е№іжҲҗ23е№ҙеҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ era|ja|дә«дҝқ|12=draft|12=draft}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=draft|12=draft}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=draft|12=draft}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|12=draft|12=draft}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=draft|12=draft}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…|12=draft|12=draft}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]]

{{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yoshino District, Nara|ja|еҗүйҮҺйғЎ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a {{ill|hot spring|ja|жё©жіү|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Totsukawa|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжқ‘|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas|ja|е№іжҲҗ23е№ҙеҸ°йўЁз¬¬12еҸ·|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ era|ja|дә«дҝқ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|kakenagashi|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Kakenagashi|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Kakenagashi (flowing baths)|6=de|7=Kakenagashi|8=fr|9=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|1=Kakenagashi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|Gojo, Nara|ja|дә”жўқеёӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Shingu, Wakayama|ja|ж–°е®®еёӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 168|ja|еӣҪйҒ“168еҸ·|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|National Route 425|ja|еӣҪйҒ“425еҸ·|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|иҝ‘з•ҝж—Ҙжң¬йү„йҒ“|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|ja|еӨ§е’Ңе…«жңЁй§…|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|JR West|ja|иҘҝж—Ҙжң¬ж—…е®ўйү„йҒ“|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Gojo Station (Nara)|ja|дә”жқЎй§…|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or {{ill|Shingu Station|ja|ж–°е®®й§…|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu|ja|еҘҲиүҜдәӨйҖҡ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Hot springs in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Springs]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:%E4%B8%8A%E6%B9%AF%E6%B8%A9%E6%B3%89]] [[en:Kami-Yu Onsen]] [[sn:ami-Yu Onsen]] [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:Kami-Yu Onsen]]
