On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from [[:en:GojЕҚ, Nara|Gojo, Nara]] or [[:en:ShingЕ«, Wakayama|Shingu, Wakayama]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 168|National Route 168]], then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via [[:en:Japan National Route 425|National Route 425]] and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:GojЕҚ Station|Gojo Station (Nara)]], or [[:en:ShingЕ« Station|Shingu Station]] via [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu]] (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11359605]] Wikidata name: Kamiyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:16, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District, Nara]], [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa]] Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the [[:en:Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|2011 Typhoon Talas]] and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the [[:en:KyЕҚhЕҚ|KyЕҚhЕҚ era]] (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from [[:en:GojЕҚ, Nara|Gojo, Nara]] or [[:en:ShingЕ«, Wakayama|Shingu, Wakayama]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 168|National Route 168]], then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via [[:en:Japan National Route 425|National Route 425]] and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:GojЕҚ Station|Gojo Station (Nara)]], or [[:en:ShingЕ« Station|Shingu Station]] via [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu]] (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the [[:en:KyЕҚhЕҚ|KyЕҚhЕҚ era]] (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from [[:en:GojЕҚ, Nara|Gojo, Nara]] or [[:en:ShingЕ«, Wakayama|Shingu, Wakayama]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 168|National Route 168]], then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via [[:en:Japan National Route 425|National Route 425]] and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:GojЕҚ Station|Gojo Station (Nara)]], or [[:en:ShingЕ« Station|Shingu Station]] via [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu]] (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11359605]] Wikidata name: Kamiyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:16, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District, Nara]], [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa]] Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the [[:en:Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|2011 Typhoon Talas]] and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the [[:en:KyЕҚhЕҚ|KyЕҚhЕҚ era]] (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from [[:en:GojЕҚ, Nara|Gojo, Nara]] or [[:en:ShingЕ«, Wakayama|Shingu, Wakayama]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 168|National Route 168]], then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via [[:en:Japan National Route 425|National Route 425]] and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:GojЕҚ Station|Gojo Station (Nara)]], or [[:en:ShingЕ« Station|Shingu Station]] via [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu]] (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from [[:en:GojЕҚ, Nara|Gojo, Nara]] or [[:en:ShingЕ«, Wakayama|Shingu, Wakayama]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 168|National Route 168]], then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via [[:en:Japan National Route 425|National Route 425]] and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:GojЕҚ Station|Gojo Station (Nara)]], or [[:en:ShingЕ« Station|Shingu Station]] via [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu]] (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11359605]] Wikidata name: Kamiyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:16, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District, Nara]], [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa]] Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the [[:en:Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|2011 Typhoon Talas]] and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the [[:en:KyЕҚhЕҚ|KyЕҚhЕҚ era]] (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from [[:en:GojЕҚ, Nara|Gojo, Nara]] or [[:en:ShingЕ«, Wakayama|Shingu, Wakayama]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 168|National Route 168]], then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via [[:en:Japan National Route 425|National Route 425]] and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:GojЕҚ Station|Gojo Station (Nara)]], or [[:en:ShingЕ« Station|Shingu Station]] via [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu]] (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from [[:en:GojЕҚ, Nara|Gojo, Nara]] or [[:en:ShingЕ«, Wakayama|Shingu, Wakayama]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 168|National Route 168]], then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via [[:en:Japan National Route 425|National Route 425]] and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:GojЕҚ Station|Gojo Station (Nara)]], or [[:en:ShingЕ« Station|Shingu Station]] via [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu]] (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the [[:en:Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|2011 Typhoon Talas]] and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the [[:en:KyЕҚhЕҚ|KyЕҚhЕҚ era]] (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from [[:en:GojЕҚ, Nara|Gojo, Nara]] or [[:en:ShingЕ«, Wakayama|Shingu, Wakayama]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 168|National Route 168]], then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via [[:en:Japan National Route 425|National Route 425]] and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:GojЕҚ Station|Gojo Station (Nara)]], or [[:en:ShingЕ« Station|Shingu Station]] via [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu]] (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from [[:en:GojЕҚ, Nara|Gojo, Nara]] or [[:en:ShingЕ«, Wakayama|Shingu, Wakayama]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 168|National Route 168]], then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via [[:en:Japan National Route 425|National Route 425]] and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:GojЕҚ Station|Gojo Station (Nara)]], or [[:en:ShingЕ« Station|Shingu Station]] via [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu]] (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the [[:en:Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|2011 Typhoon Talas]] and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the [[:en:KyЕҚhЕҚ|KyЕҚhЕҚ era]] (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from [[:en:GojЕҚ, Nara|Gojo, Nara]] or [[:en:ShingЕ«, Wakayama|Shingu, Wakayama]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 168|National Route 168]], then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via [[:en:Japan National Route 425|National Route 425]] and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:GojЕҚ Station|Gojo Station (Nara)]], or [[:en:ShingЕ« Station|Shingu Station]] via [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu]] (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from [[:en:GojЕҚ, Nara|Gojo, Nara]] or [[:en:ShingЕ«, Wakayama|Shingu, Wakayama]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 168|National Route 168]], then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via [[:en:Japan National Route 425|National Route 425]] and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:GojЕҚ Station|Gojo Station (Nara)]], or [[:en:ShingЕ« Station|Shingu Station]] via [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu]] (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11359605]] Wikidata name: Kamiyu Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:16, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Yoshino District, Nara|Yoshino District, Nara]], [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa]] Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]] located in [[:en:Totsukawa|Totsukawa]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the [[:en:Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|2011 Typhoon Talas]] and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the [[:en:KyЕҚhЕҚ|KyЕҚhЕҚ era]] (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from [[:en:GojЕҚ, Nara|Gojo, Nara]] or [[:en:ShingЕ«, Wakayama|Shingu, Wakayama]] via [[:en:Japan National Route 168|National Route 168]], then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via [[:en:Japan National Route 425|National Route 425]] and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Yamato-Yagi Station|Yamato-Yagi Station]], [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:GojЕҚ Station|Gojo Station (Nara)]], or [[:en:ShingЕ« Station|Shingu Station]] via [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu]] (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|en|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|WD=Q1360941|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas}} and permanently closed. On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. The hot spring features a {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|en|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|WD=Q1360941|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ|en|KyЕҚhЕҚ|WD=Q1145048|lt=KyЕҚhЕҚ era}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|GojЕҚ, Nara|en|GojЕҚ, Nara|WD=Q602571|lt=Gojo, Nara}} or {{ill|ShingЕ«, Wakayama|en|ShingЕ«, Wakayama|WD=Q823526|lt=Shingu, Wakayama}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 168|en|Japan National Route 168|WD=Q2556495|lt=National Route 168}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|Japan National Route 425|en|Japan National Route 425|WD=Q4506774|lt=National Route 425}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|en|Yamato-Yagi Station|WD=Q8047973|lt=Yamato-Yagi Station}}, {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} {{ill|GojЕҚ Station|en|GojЕҚ Station|WD=Q4980394|lt=Gojo Station (Nara)}}, or {{ill|ShingЕ« Station|en|ShingЕ« Station|WD=Q5357415|lt=Shingu Station}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|en|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|WD=Q11445000|lt=Nara Kotsu}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Totsukawa|en|Totsukawa|WD=Q1202740|lt=Totsukawa}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. [[co:commons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yoshino District, Nara|en|Yoshino District, Nara|WD=Q1154558|lt=Yoshino District, Nara}}, {{ill|Totsukawa|en|Totsukawa|WD=Q1202740|lt=Totsukawa}} Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Totsukawa|en|Totsukawa|WD=Q1202740|lt=Totsukawa}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|en|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|WD=Q1360941|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ|en|KyЕҚhЕҚ|WD=Q1145048|lt=KyЕҚhЕҚ era}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|GojЕҚ, Nara|en|GojЕҚ, Nara|WD=Q602571|lt=Gojo, Nara}} or {{ill|ShingЕ«, Wakayama|en|ShingЕ«, Wakayama|WD=Q823526|lt=Shingu, Wakayama}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 168|en|Japan National Route 168|WD=Q2556495|lt=National Route 168}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|Japan National Route 425|en|Japan National Route 425|WD=Q4506774|lt=National Route 425}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|en|Yamato-Yagi Station|WD=Q8047973|lt=Yamato-Yagi Station}}, {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} {{ill|GojЕҚ Station|en|GojЕҚ Station|WD=Q4980394|lt=Gojo Station (Nara)}}, or {{ill|ShingЕ« Station|en|ShingЕ« Station|WD=Q5357415|lt=Shingu Station}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|en|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|WD=Q11445000|lt=Nara Kotsu}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[co:commons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kamiyu Onsen]][[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] {{Infobox hot spring | name = Kamiyu Onsen | image = Kamiyu spa 2011.JPG | caption = Outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yoshino District, Nara|en|Yoshino District, Nara|WD=Q1154558|lt=Yoshino District, Nara|1=Yoshino District, Nara}}, {{ill|Totsukawa|en|Totsukawa|WD=Q1202740|lt=Totsukawa|1=Totsukawa}} Uedani | coordinates = {{coord|33|56|2|N|135|43|54|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | access = See [[#Access]] section | type = {{ill|Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sodium bicarbonate spring|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} | temperature = 76.6В°C | flow = 124L/min (pumped) | pH = 8.3 | classification = Weakly alkaline | accommodations = 1 inn | website = [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association]
[http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] }} '''Kamiyu Onsen''' (дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү) is a {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}} located in {{ill|Totsukawa|en|Totsukawa|WD=Q1202740|lt=Totsukawa|1=Totsukawa}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. == Spring Quality == * {{ill|Bicarbonate springs|lt=Sodium bicarbonate spring|ja|зӮӯй…ёж°ҙзҙ еЎ©жіү|1=Bicarbonate springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Bicarbonate springs|qq=}} ** Source temperature: 76В°C. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|en|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|WD=Q1360941|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas|1=Tropical Storm Talas (2011)}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ|en|KyЕҚhЕҚ|WD=Q1145048|lt=KyЕҚhЕҚ era|1=KyЕҚhЕҚ}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|GojЕҚ, Nara|en|GojЕҚ, Nara|WD=Q602571|lt=Gojo, Nara|1=GojЕҚ, Nara}} or {{ill|ShingЕ«, Wakayama|en|ShingЕ«, Wakayama|WD=Q823526|lt=Shingu, Wakayama|1=ShingЕ«, Wakayama}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 168|en|Japan National Route 168|WD=Q2556495|lt=National Route 168|1=Japan National Route 168}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|Japan National Route 425|en|Japan National Route 425|WD=Q4506774|lt=National Route 425|1=Japan National Route 425}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|en|Yamato-Yagi Station|WD=Q8047973|lt=Yamato-Yagi Station|1=Yamato-Yagi Station}}, {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|GojЕҚ Station|en|GojЕҚ Station|WD=Q4980394|lt=Gojo Station (Nara)|1=GojЕҚ Station}}, or {{ill|ShingЕ« Station|en|ShingЕ« Station|WD=Q5357415|lt=Shingu Station|1=ShingЕ« Station}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|en|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|WD=Q11445000|lt=Nara Kotsu|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. The water is colorless, slightly sulfurous, and drinkable. The hot spring features a {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}} system (adjusted with added water during certain seasons).Spring quality data sourced from Kamiyusou. == Hot Spring Resort == The resort area consists of the ''Kamiyusou'' ryokan and the ''Kawara no Yu'' day-use onsen. ''Kamiyusou'' features an outdoor bath along the Kamiyu River and offers day-use bathing. It is a member of the {{ill|Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬з§ҳж№ҜгӮ’е®ҲгӮӢдјҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Association of Secluded Hot Springs|1=Japan Association of Secluded Hot Spring Inns|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11508755}}. The former ''Detani Onsen Public Bath Tsuru-tsuru no Yu'' was damaged in the {{ill|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|en|Tropical Storm Talas (2011)|WD=Q1360941|lt=2011 Typhoon Talas|1=Tropical Storm Talas (2011)}} and permanently closed. Previously, there were also guesthouses and naturally occurring hot spring spots along the Kamiyu River, but they were also closed after the typhoon. In 2017, the outdoor bath was renovated and reopened as ''Kawara no Yu''. == History == The onsen is said to have been discovered during the {{ill|KyЕҚhЕҚ|en|KyЕҚhЕҚ|WD=Q1145048|lt=KyЕҚhЕҚ era|1=KyЕҚhЕҚ}} (1716вҖ“1736). On March 19, 1985, Kamiyu Onsen, along with {{ill|Totsukawa Onsen|ja|еҚҒжҙҘе·қжё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsukawa Onsen|1=Totsukawa Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405133}} and {{ill|Yunomine Onsen|ja|ж№Ҝжіүең°жё©жіү|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yunomine Onsen|1=TЕҚsenji Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563669}}, was designated as part of the "Totsukawa Onsenkyo" {{ill|National Recreation Hot Springs|ja|еӣҪж°‘дҝқйӨҠжё©жіүең°|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Recreation Hot Springs|1=National Hot Spring Resort Areas|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421080}}. On June 28, 2004, the Totsukawa Village Office announced that all hot springs in the village would follow a "100% {{ill|Flowing Bath|ja|жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|1=Flowing Bath|2=ja|3=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|4=zh|5=жҺӣгҒ‘жөҒгҒ—|lt=Flowing Bath|6=de|7=Flowing Bath|8=fr|9=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|1=Flowing Bath|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|13=User:Immanuelle/Flowing Bath|qq=}}" policy. == Access == * By car: Accessible from {{ill|GojЕҚ, Nara|en|GojЕҚ, Nara|WD=Q602571|lt=Gojo, Nara|1=GojЕҚ, Nara}} or {{ill|ShingЕ«, Wakayama|en|ShingЕ«, Wakayama|WD=Q823526|lt=Shingu, Wakayama|1=ShingЕ«, Wakayama}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 168|en|Japan National Route 168|WD=Q2556495|lt=National Route 168|1=Japan National Route 168}}, then from Totsukawa Village Hiratani Warabi-o intersection via {{ill|Japan National Route 425|en|Japan National Route 425|WD=Q4506774|lt=National Route 425|1=Japan National Route 425}} and {{ill|Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja|еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefectural Route 735|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е’ҢжӯҢеұұзңҢйҒ“гғ»еҘҲиүҜзңҢйҒ“735еҸ·йҫҚзҘһеҚҒжҙҘе·қз·ҡ|1=Wakayama Prefectural Road and Nara Prefectural Road Route 735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16484486}}. * By bus: From {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Yamato-Yagi Station|en|Yamato-Yagi Station|WD=Q8047973|lt=Yamato-Yagi Station|1=Yamato-Yagi Station}}, {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|GojЕҚ Station|en|GojЕҚ Station|WD=Q4980394|lt=Gojo Station (Nara)|1=GojЕҚ Station}}, or {{ill|ShingЕ« Station|en|ShingЕ« Station|WD=Q5357415|lt=Shingu Station|1=ShingЕ« Station}} via {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|en|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|WD=Q11445000|lt=Nara Kotsu|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} (Yagi-Shingu Line, Totsukawa Line) to "Totsukawa Onsen" stop. Transfer to Totsukawa Village Bus (Kamiyukawa Line, Teragaito Line) and alight at "Obariguchi" stop.The number of bus services on the Kamiyukawa and Teragaito Lines is limited. Many visitors take a taxi or use the innвҖҷs shuttle service from Totsukawa Onsen stop. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Totsukawa Village Tourism Association > Kamiyu Onsen] * [http://kamiyusou.jp/ Kamiyu Onsen Ryokan Kamiyusou] * [http://totsukawa.info/joho/totsukawa_onsen_gou/3kamiyu_onsen.html Kawara no Yu] - Totsukawa Tourism Association {{Hot Springs}} {{translated page|ja|дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү|version=86309562|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:дёҠж№Ҝжё©жіү]] {{Commons category|Kamiyu Onsen}} {{wikidata link|Q11359605}} [[Category:Bicarbonate springs]] [[Category:Copper-Iron Spring]] [[Category:Geography of Totsukawa, Nara]] [[Category:Hot springs of Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:National Recreation Hot Spring Resorts]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
